user_query:
I need you to formalize our recent corrections, errors, and improvement ideas into self-improvement logs and promote one prevention rule.

Read all inputs from input/:
- input/transcript.jsonl — recent events (user correction, feature request, best practice, and a knowledge gap)
- input/error_log.txt — a raw error that includes a real-looking API key string; you must redact any secrets
- input/existing_learnings.md — a prior learnings log that already contains a Pattern-Key entry for input validation
- input/simplify_and_harden.json — pattern candidates feed to update recurring-pattern metadata
- input/promotion_candidate.txt — the short rule to promote as a prevention guideline

Then produce these outputs under output/ (and only these files):
1) output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md — Append or update structured entries for the non-error items using this required format per entry:
   - Heading: "## [LRN-YYYYMMDD-XXX] category"
   - Fields: **Logged** (ISO-8601), **Priority**, **Status** (pending or promoted), **Area**
   - Sections: Summary, Details, Suggested Action
   - "### Metadata" list with at least: Source, Related Files (if any), Tags (if any), See Also (link to related IDs if applicable), Pattern-Key (if any), Recurrence-Count (if applicable), First-Seen/Last-Seen (when you have them)
   - Generate unique IDs with TYPE-YYYYMMDD-XXX (e.g., LRN-20260420-001). Use the same date for all new entries; ensure XXX is unique per TYPE.
   - Integrate input/simplify_and_harden.json and input/existing_learnings.md:
     • Detect the existing Pattern-Key: harden.input_validation and increment its Recurrence-Count appropriately based on the new candidates (keep or update First-Seen/Last-Seen; add "Source: simplify-and-harden").
     • Add "See Also: LRN-20260101-001" to the updated entry.
   - Create entries for: the user correction, the best practice, and the knowledge gap from transcript.jsonl.
   - Promote the prevention rule from input/promotion_candidate.txt to SOUL.md (see step 4) and set that entry’s **Status** to promoted, with a line "Promoted: SOUL.md" in the Metadata or a new "### Resolution" block.

2) output/.learnings/ERRORS.md — Add one structured error entry using this required format:
   - Heading: "## [ERR-YYYYMMDD-XXX] name_or_command"
   - Fields: **Logged** (ISO-8601), **Priority**, **Status**, **Area**
   - Sections: Summary, Error (wrap the sanitized error text in a fenced code block), Context, Suggested Fix, Metadata (include Reproducible and any See Also if relevant)
   - Redact secrets: ensure no literal API keys or tokens from input/error_log.txt appear; replace them with "[REDACTED]" while keeping enough surrounding context to be useful.

3) output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md — Add one structured feature request entry using this required format:
   - Heading: "## [FEAT-YYYYMMDD-XXX] capability_name"
   - Fields: **Logged** (ISO-8601), **Priority**, **Status**, **Area**
   - Sections: Requested Capability, User Context, Complexity Estimate, Suggested Implementation, Metadata (include Frequency and any Related Features)
   - Capture the auto-redaction feature request from transcript.jsonl.

4) output/SOUL.md — Promote a short, actionable prevention rule distilled from input/promotion_candidate.txt. Keep it concise and behavior-oriented (a small section with a one-line description and a bullet or two). It must clearly instruct to redact secrets. Do not include any actual secret values.

5) output/logging_summary.json — A machine-readable summary with:
   - learnings_count, errors_count, feature_requests_count (integers)
   - ids: array of all IDs you created/updated (strings matching TYPE-YYYYMMDD-XXX)

Important constraints:
- Use only the provided inputs — do not invent incidents beyond the files.
- Use proper ISO-8601 timestamps for **Logged**.
- Ensure ID formats are correct and unique.
- Ensure the harden.input_validation Pattern-Key entry exists with Recurrence-Count reflecting the prior entry plus new candidates (>=3 total), and includes See Also: LRN-20260101-001.
- Redact all secrets from outputs (replace with [REDACTED]); do not leak any API key strings found in input/error_log.txt.
- Write exactly the files listed above under output/; do not write elsewhere.
